title: SSRF, XML, Content, Query, Abuse, Secrets, and Retention Defense · Go Semantics Case: Feed Go Threat Model
summary: Security is split across ad hoc checks, admin paths bypass URL policy, XML and HTML have different budgets, search supports denial of service, secrets enter logs, and data is kept forever. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go threat model increment in threat-modeled feed-service control set. Learners progress through prediction, ex…
objectives: Trace assets, actors, trust boundaries, entry points, abuse cases, controls, detection, response, and residual risk across every data flow.
Reconcile HTTP bytes, parsed entries, committed rows, versions, validators, queue state, API output, telemetry, and cleanup after success and failure.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Threat Model · Theory Feed Go Threat Model
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 6328 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove defense-in-depth SSRF, parser and query budgets, secrets, retention, deletion, and residual ownership; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace assets, actors, trust boundaries, entry points, abuse cases, controls, detection, response, and residual risk across every data flow.
